title: OpenAI is trying to conquer the office. Legal is next.
excerpt: For years, OpenAI let startups like Harvey build legal software on top of its AI. Now, the company is making moves to court lawyers directly.
content_html:
For years, OpenAI let startups like Harvey, Legora, and others build software for lawyers on top of its foundation models — massive artificial intelligence systems trained on broad swaths of data. Now, the company, last valued at $852 billion, is making moves to court lawyers directly.

OpenAI signaled that shift in June when it hired Jason Boehmig, an executive who spent more than a decade figuring out the slow, messy business of selling software to lawyers. He had left a Big Law job to start Ironclad, a company that helps corporate legal departments manage contracts.

That business looks a lot sexier than it did when Boehmig spun out. Law firms are under pressure from clients to move faster and show they are not billing armies of associates for work that chatbots can do. In-house legal teams are looking for ways to automate more work themselves, so they can send less to outside counsel and lower their legal bills.

Suddenly, artificial intelligence has turned legal tech from what Boehmig once called an investor "wasteland" into one of venture capital's hottest parties. And OpenAI wants in.

"Jason's hire reflects the value OpenAI sees in the legal space," Dan Springer, who took over as Ironclad's chief executive last year, said in an email.

The first hints of a playbook are now emerging. On Wednesday, OpenAI told Bloomberg Law that it is collaborating with the white-shoe law firm Willkie Farr & Gallagher to roll out ChatGPT Enterprise to every attorney and fold its large language models deeper into the firm's own tools.

OpenAI's legal entry is part of a broader campaign to make ChatGPT NSFW. The company has built a desktop "super app" for software engineers, introduced secure healthcare tools for clinicians, and hired former investment bankers to train its systems in the ways of Wall Street.

The stakes are enormous. OpenAI has filed paperwork to go public, and it wants to show investors that ChatGPT is not merely the thing people use to write emails, self-diagnose a rash, or ask whether their landlord can really do that. It wants to become the place where knowledge workers do their jobs, as well as the world's most popular chatbot.

In legal, that means taking on Anthropic, which has spent all year trying to make Claude as familiar to lawyers as a yellow legal pad. In January, the company released a legal work plugin that could analyze contracts and track compliance, frightening investors in legacy legal software and sending shares of Thomson Reuters, Relx, and Wolters Kluwer sharply lower.

For now, little is known about what OpenAI will actually build. In a LinkedIn post about his move, Boehmig said he would be working on products for the legal industry. A job posting for a founding software engineer on OpenAI's website is similarly broad, stating that the company aims to "transform legal workflows, improve access to information, and enable legal professionals and organizations to work more effectively." OpenAI declined to comment for this story.

The question of what it builds is an existential one for legal startups. A technology that combs through contracts for risks poses a greater threat to a business built around contracts, whereas a bundle of legal skills strikes closer to Harvey's turf. OpenAI was an early Harvey investor.

In interviews, Sam Altman has often described his own company as a foundation for other startups to build on, rather than a competitor with an app for every industry. "We see a future where intelligence is a utility like electricity or water," he said onstage at a BlackRock event in March, "and people buy it from us on a meter and use it for whatever they want to use it for."

Legal may show whether OpenAI is content to sell the electricity — or whether it wants to manufacture the appliances, too.

It's in the water

An open question is whether OpenAI is too late to a category its main rival has already been working on.

"Absolutely not," Zach Abramowitz, a consultant who advises law firms on how technology is disrupting their industry, said in an interview.

His reasoning is simple: Lawyers may not be buying "ChatGPT for Law" yet, but many of them are already ChatGPT power users. A recent Law360 survey found that attorneys are using AI a lot more often than they were a year ago. The tool with the highest self-reported usage was ChatGPT, at 54% of respondents, ahead of Microsoft Copilot, Gemini, Harvey, and Claude.

In-house adoption is harder to track, but the pull may be even stronger. Corporate legal teams have every incentive to automate more work, and many sit inside companies where ChatGPT or Claude is already being used by engineers, customer support, and everyone in between.

Lawyers were among the millions of users who road-tested ChatGTP in those first weeks after its 2022 release. They tried asking it for legal advice (answers ranged from "surprisingly good" to "dangerously wrong") and put it through the bar exam (it scored 50.3% correct, above the random guessing rate). The overall results were not "that impressive for a human lawyer," as one attorney wrote at the time, but were "pretty amazing" for a machine.

Together, these experiments pointed to something many attorneys hadn't considered seriously before, law firm executive Oz Benamram writes in his new book, "Law, Reinvented." Artificial intelligence was becoming good enough to take on parts of legal work.

Tech founders had their own wake-up call.

That year, Harvey's founders cold-emailed Jason Kwon, OpenAI's general counsel at the time, convinced that the models could be tuned to make legal research, drafting, and other lawyerly tasks ridiculously faster. Today, Harvey serves more than 100,000 legal professionals and is valued at $11 billion.

Harvey became the proof point for a bigger gold rush. From San Francisco to Stockholm, founders raced to package large language models as apps for lawyers. Funding to legal tech startups worldwide reached $2.1 billion in the first half of 2026, per Crunchbase data.

Anthropic jumped headlong into the category. In May, the rival lab released Claude for Legal, a library of plugins aimed at law firms and in-house legal counsel. It targeted the grunt work of the profession: finding cases, reviewing contracts, and routing documents and requests.

Meanwhile, the company that helped create the legal tech boom largely sat out the next chapter.

OpenAI has sold ChatGPT Enterprise to a growing number of law firms, and last year, it blogged about an internal tool its employees used to spot issues and pull information from contracts. But that system was built to help OpenAI run its business and not released as a product for lawyers.

'Welcome to the party'

Boehmig joined OpenAI seven weeks ago. He's already putting a band together.

In July, Stacey Wang left Ironclad for OpenAI to lead industry marketing for legal and professional services. In social media posts about their moves, both said they're working closely with Sherwin Wu, who runs engineering for B2B Verticals at OpenAI Platform, the business that lets other companies build products using OpenAI's models.

OpenAI has also been staffing people to answer a more basic question: Are its models actually good enough for legal work? In April, the company hired a former corporate attorney as a legal evaluations specialist. AI labs use evaluations to measure how well their systems perform on real-world tasks, so they can tell whether each new version is actually getting better.

The hires have sharpened a fear that has been building across legal tech. The model companies may not be content to power everyone else's software. They may want the workflow, too.

Winston Weinberg, Harvey's founder and chief executive, is already used to living with the tension. Even before OpenAI began staffing up for legal, Weinberg told Business Insider that Harvey would always be compared with whatever broad-use tool OpenAI shipped last.

On a recent call with Business Insider, Weinberg seemed coolheaded, even as Harvey prepares for a future in which one of its most important partners could become a more direct challenger. His view is that every new entrant helps Harvey by drawing more attention to legal AI and showing lawyers what the technology can do.

His message for OpenAI: "Welcome to the party."

Read next

Melia Russell

The tool with the highest self-reported usage was ChatGPT, at 54% of respondents, ahead of Microsoft Copilot, Gemini, Harvey, and Claude.

sentences.en: His reasoning is simple: Lawyers may not be buying "ChatGPT for Law" yet, but many of them are already ChatGPT power users.
sentences.zh: 他的理由很简单：律师可能还没有购买“ChatGPT for Law”，但他们中的许多人已经是 ChatGPT 的高级用户。

sentences.en: A recent Law360 survey found that attorneys are using AI a lot more often than they were a year ago.
sentences.zh: Law360 最近的一项调查发现，律师使用人工智能的频率比一年前要高得多。

sentences.en: The tool with the highest self-reported usage was ChatGPT, at 54% of respondents, ahead of Microsoft Copilot, Gemini, Harvey, and Claude.
sentences.zh: 自我报告使用率最高的工具是 ChatGPT，占受访者的 54%，领先于 Microsoft Copilot、Gemini、Harvey 和 Claude。

blocks.html_en: In-house adoption is harder to track, but the pull may be even stronger. Corporate legal teams have every incentive to automate more work, and many sit inside companies where ChatGPT or Claude is already being used by engineers, customer support, and everyone in between.

sentences.en: In-house adoption is harder to track, but the pull may be even stronger.
sentences.zh: 内部采用情况更难追踪，但吸引力可能更大。

sentences.en: Corporate legal teams have every incentive to automate more work, and many sit inside companies where ChatGPT or Claude is already being used by engineers, customer support, and everyone in between.
sentences.zh: 公司法律团队有充分的动力将更多工作自动化，而且许多人都坐在公司内部，工程师、客户支持以及中间的每个人都已经在使用 ChatGPT 或 Claude。

blocks.html_en: Lawyers were among the millions of users who road-tested ChatGTP in those first weeks after its 2022 release. They tried asking it for legal advice (answers ranged from "surprisingly good" to "dangerously wrong") and put it through the bar exam (it scored 50.3% correct, above the random guessing rate). The overall results were not "that impressive for a human lawyer," as one attorney wrote at the time, but were "pretty amazing" for a machine.

sentences.en: Lawyers were among the millions of users who road-tested ChatGTP in those first weeks after its 2022 release.
sentences.zh: ChatGTP 于 2022 年发布后的最初几周内，数百万用户中包括律师在内。

sentences.en: They tried asking it for legal advice (answers ranged from "surprisingly good" to "dangerously wrong") and put it through the bar exam (it scored 50.3% correct, above the random guessing rate).
sentences.zh: 他们尝试向其寻求法律建议（答案范围从“令人惊讶的好”到“危险的错误”）并通过律师资格考试（其正确率达到 50.3%，高于随机猜测率）。

sentences.en: The overall results were not "that impressive for a human lawyer," as one attorney wrote at the time, but were "pretty amazing" for a machine.
sentences.zh: 正如一位律师当时所写的那样，总体结果“对于人类律师来说并不令人印象深刻”，但对于机器来说“相当惊人”。

blocks.html_en: Together, these experiments pointed to something many attorneys hadn't considered seriously before, law firm executive Oz Benamram writes in his new book, "Law, Reinvented." Artificial intelligence was becoming good enough to take on parts of legal work.

sentences.en: Together, these experiments pointed to something many attorneys hadn't considered seriously before, law firm executive Oz Benamram writes in his new book, "Law, Reinvented."
sentences.zh: 律师事务所高管 Oz Benamram 在他的新书《法律，重塑》中写道，这些实验共同指出了许多律师以前没有认真考虑过的事情。

sentences.en: Artificial intelligence was becoming good enough to take on parts of legal work.
sentences.zh: 人工智能已经足够好，可以承担部分法律工作。

sentences.en: Winston Weinberg says OpenAI's jump into the legal AI space is a net positive for Harvey.
sentences.zh: Winston Weinberg 表示 OpenAI 进军合法人工智能领域对 Harvey 来说是一个积极的因素。

sentences.en: Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images
sentences.zh: 凯文迪奇/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: Tech founders had their own wake-up call.

sentences.en: Tech founders had their own wake-up call.
sentences.zh: 科技创始人也敲响了警钟。

blocks.html_en: That year, Harvey's founders cold-emailed Jason Kwon, OpenAI's general counsel at the time, convinced that the models could be tuned to make legal research, drafting, and other lawyerly tasks ridiculously faster. Today, Harvey serves more than 100,000 legal professionals and is valued at $11 billion.

sentences.en: That year, Harvey's founders cold-emailed Jason Kwon, OpenAI's general counsel at the time, convinced that the models could be tuned to make legal research, drafting, and other lawyerly tasks ridiculously faster.
sentences.zh: 那一年，Harvey 的创始人给当时 OpenAI 的总法律顾问 Jason Kwon 发了一封冷邮件，相信这些模型可以经过调整，使法律研究、起草和其他律师任务的速度快得离谱。

sentences.en: Today, Harvey serves more than 100,000 legal professionals and is valued at $11 billion.
sentences.zh: 如今，Harvey 为超过 100,000 名法律专业人士提供服务，估值达 110 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Harvey became the proof point for a bigger gold rush. From San Francisco to Stockholm, founders raced to package large language models as apps for lawyers. Funding to legal tech startups worldwide reached $2.1 billion in the first half of 2026, per Crunchbase data.

sentences.en: Harvey became the proof point for a bigger gold rush.
sentences.zh: 哈维成为了更大的淘金热的证据。

sentences.en: From San Francisco to Stockholm, founders raced to package large language models as apps for lawyers.
sentences.zh: 从旧金山到斯德哥尔摩，创始人竞相将大型语言模型打包为律师应用程序。

sentences.en: Funding to legal tech startups worldwide reached $2.1 billion in the first half of 2026, per Crunchbase data.
sentences.zh: 根据 Crunchbase 的数据，2026 年上半年，全球法律科技初创公司的融资额达到 21 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic jumped headlong into the category. In May, the rival lab released Claude for Legal, a library of plugins aimed at law firms and in-house legal counsel. It targeted the grunt work of the profession: finding cases, reviewing contracts, and routing documents and requests.

sentences.en: Anthropic jumped headlong into the category.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 一头扎进了这一类别。

sentences.en: In May, the rival lab released Claude for Legal, a library of plugins aimed at law firms and in-house legal counsel.
sentences.zh: 五月份，竞争对手实验室发布了 Claude for Legal，这是一个针对律师事务所和内部法律顾问的插件库。

sentences.en: It targeted the grunt work of the profession: finding cases, reviewing contracts, and routing documents and requests.
sentences.zh: 它针对的是该行业的繁重工作：查找案例、审查合同以及传送文件和请求。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, the company that helped create the legal tech boom largely sat out the next chapter.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, the company that helped create the legal tech boom largely sat out the next chapter.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，帮助创造法律技术繁荣的公司基本上没有参与下一章。

blocks.html_en: OpenAI has sold ChatGPT Enterprise to a growing number of law firms, and last year, it blogged about an internal tool its employees used to spot issues and pull information from contracts. But that system was built to help OpenAI run its business and not released as a product for lawyers.

sentences.en: OpenAI has sold ChatGPT Enterprise to a growing number of law firms, and last year, it blogged about an internal tool its employees used to spot issues and pull information from contracts.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 已将 ChatGPT Enterprise 出售给越来越多的律师事务所，去年，该公司在博客中介绍了其员工用来发现问题并从合同中提取信息的内部工具。

sentences.en: But that system was built to help OpenAI run its business and not released as a product for lawyers.
sentences.zh: 但该系统是为了帮助 OpenAI 运营其业务而构建的，而不是作为律师产品发布。

blocks.html_en: 'Welcome to the party'

sentences.en: 'Welcome to the party'
sentences.zh: “欢迎来到聚会”

blocks.html_en: Boehmig joined OpenAI seven weeks ago. He's already putting a band together.

sentences.en: Boehmig joined OpenAI seven weeks ago.
sentences.zh: Boehmig 七周前加入 OpenAI。

sentences.en: He's already putting a band together.
sentences.zh: 他已经组建了一支乐队。

blocks.html_en: In July, Stacey Wang left Ironclad for OpenAI to lead industry marketing for legal and professional services. In social media posts about their moves, both said they're working closely with Sherwin Wu, who runs engineering for B2B Verticals at OpenAI Platform, the business that lets other companies build products using OpenAI's models.

sentences.en: In July, Stacey Wang left Ironclad for OpenAI to lead industry marketing for legal and professional services.
sentences.zh: 7 月，Stacey Wang 离开 Ironclad 前往 OpenAI，领导法律和专业服务的行业营销。

sentences.en: In social media posts about their moves, both said they're working closely with Sherwin Wu, who runs engineering for B2B Verticals at OpenAI Platform, the business that lets other companies build products using OpenAI's models.
sentences.zh: 在社交媒体帖子中，两人都表示正在与 Sherwin Wu 密切合作，Sherwin Wu 在 OpenAI Platform 负责 B2B Verticals 的工程工作，该业务允许其他公司使用 OpenAI 的模型构建产品。

sentences.en: Jason Boehmig.
sentences.zh: 贾森·伯米格。

sentences.en: Paul Devlin/Getty Images
sentences.zh: 保罗·德夫林/盖蒂图片社

blocks.html_en: OpenAI has also been staffing people to answer a more basic question: Are its models actually good enough for legal work? In April, the company hired a former corporate attorney as a legal evaluations specialist. AI labs use evaluations to measure how well their systems perform on real-world tasks, so they can tell whether each new version is actually getting better.

sentences.en: OpenAI has also been staffing people to answer a more basic question: Are its models actually good enough for legal work?
sentences.zh: OpenAI 还配备了人员来回答一个更基本的问题：它的模型是否真的足以胜任法律工作？

sentences.en: In April, the company hired a former corporate attorney as a legal evaluations specialist.
sentences.zh: 4月，该公司聘请了一位前公司律师作为法律评估专家。

sentences.en: AI labs use evaluations to measure how well their systems perform on real-world tasks, so they can tell whether each new version is actually getting better.
sentences.zh: 人工智能实验室使用评估来衡量他们的系统在现实世界任务中的表现，这样他们就可以判断每个新版本是否真的变得更好。

blocks.html_en: The hires have sharpened a fear that has been building across legal tech. The model companies may not be content to power everyone else's software. They may want the workflow, too.

sentences.en: The hires have sharpened a fear that has been building across legal tech.
sentences.zh: 这些招聘加剧了法律科技领域一直存在的恐惧。

sentences.en: The model companies may not be content to power everyone else's software.
sentences.zh: 模范公司可能不满足于为其他人的软件提供支持。

sentences.en: They may want the workflow, too.
sentences.zh: 他们可能也想要工作流程。

blocks.html_en: Winston Weinberg, Harvey's founder and chief executive, is already used to living with the tension. Even before OpenAI began staffing up for legal, Weinberg told Business Insider that Harvey would always be compared with whatever broad-use tool OpenAI shipped last.

sentences.en: Winston Weinberg, Harvey's founder and chief executive, is already used to living with the tension.
sentences.zh: Harvey 的创始人兼首席执行官温斯顿·温伯格 (Winston Weinberg) 已经习惯了这种紧张气氛。

sentences.en: Even before OpenAI began staffing up for legal, Weinberg told Business Insider that Harvey would always be compared with whatever broad-use tool OpenAI shipped last.
sentences.zh: 甚至在 OpenAI 开始配备法律人员之前，温伯格就告诉《商业内幕》，Harvey 总是会与 OpenAI 最后发布的任何广泛使用的工具进行比较。

blocks.html_en: On a recent call with Business Insider, Weinberg seemed coolheaded, even as Harvey prepares for a future in which one of its most important partners could become a more direct challenger. His view is that every new entrant helps Harvey by drawing more attention to legal AI and showing lawyers what the technology can do.

sentences.en: On a recent call with Business Insider, Weinberg seemed coolheaded, even as Harvey prepares for a future in which one of its most important partners could become a more direct challenger.
sentences.zh: 在最近接受《商业内幕》采访时，温伯格似乎很冷静，尽管哈维正在为其最重要的合作伙伴之一可能成为更直接挑战者的未来做准备。

sentences.en: His view is that every new entrant helps Harvey by drawing more attention to legal AI and showing lawyers what the technology can do.
sentences.zh: 他的观点是，每个新进入者都可以通过吸引更多人关注法律人工智能并向律师展示该技术的用途来帮助哈维。

blocks.html_en: His message for OpenAI: "Welcome to the party."

sentences.en: His message for OpenAI: "Welcome to the party."
sentences.zh: 他向 OpenAI 传达的信息是：“欢迎来到聚会。”

sentences.en: Read next
sentences.zh: 阅读下一篇

blocks.html_en: • OpenAI

sentences.en: OpenAI
sentences.zh: 开放人工智能

blocks.html_en: • ChatGPT

sentences.en: ChatGPT
sentences.zh: 聊天GPT

blocks.html_en: • AI
• More

content_html_zh:
多年来，OpenAI 让 Harvey、Legora 等初创公司在其基础模型（经过大量数据训练的大型人工智能系统）之上为律师构建软件。 现在，该公司的最新估值为 8520 亿美元，正在直接向律师提起诉讼。

OpenAI 在 6 月份聘请了杰森·博米格 (Jason Boehmig)，标志着这一转变。他是一位高管，花了十多年的时间来解决向律师销售软件这一缓慢而混乱的业务。 他辞去了 Big Law 的工作，创办了 Ironclad，这是一家帮助企业法律部门管理合同的公司。

这项业务看起来比伯米格分拆时更有吸引力。 律师事务所面临来自客户的压力，要求他们加快行动，并表明他们不会向大批律师收取聊天机器人可以完成的工作费用。 内部法律团队正在寻找使更多工作自动化的方法，这样他们就可以减少交给外部顾问的工作并降低法律费用。

突然之间，人工智能已经将法律技术从伯米格曾经称之为投资者“荒地”的地方变成了风险投资最热门的领域之一。 OpenAI 也想加入。

去年接任 Ironclad 首席执行官的 Dan Springer 在一封电子邮件中表示：“Jason 的聘用反映了 OpenAI 在法律领域看到的价值。”

剧本的第一个线索现在正在浮现。 周三，OpenAI 告诉 Bloomberg Law，它正在与白鞋律师事务所 Willkie Farr & Gallagher 合作，向每位律师推出 ChatGPT Enterprise，并将其大型语言模型更深入地融入公司自己的工具中。

OpenAI 的合法进入是使 ChatGPT NSFW 更广泛的活动的一部分。 该公司为软件工程师构建了桌面“超级应用程序”，为临床医生推出了安全的医疗保健工具，并聘请了前投资银行家以华尔街的方式培训其系统。

赌注是巨大的。 OpenAI 已提交上市文件，它希望向投资者展示 ChatGPT 不仅仅是人们用来写电子邮件、自我诊断皮疹或询问房东是否真的可以做到这一点的工具。 它希望成为知识工作者工作的地方，以及世界上最受欢迎的聊天机器人。

在法律领域，这意味着与 Anthropic 较量，该公司花了一整年的时间试图让克劳德像黄色便笺簿一样被律师们所熟悉。 一月份，该公司发布了一个法律工作插件，可以分析合同并跟踪合规情况，吓坏了传统法律软件的投资者，并导致汤森路透、Relx 和 Wolters Kluwer 的股价大幅下跌。

目前，人们对 OpenAI 实际构建的内容知之甚少。 博米格在 LinkedIn 上发布了一篇关于他的举动的帖子，称他将致力于为法律行业开发产品。 OpenAI 网站上针对一位创始软件工程师的招聘信息也同样宽泛，称该公司的目标是“转变法律工作流程，改善信息获取，并使法律专业人士和组织能够更有效地工作”。 OpenAI 拒绝对本文发表评论。

对于合法初创企业来说，它要构建什么是一个关乎生存的问题。 梳理合同风险的技术对围绕合同建立的业务构成了更大的威胁，而一系列法律技能则更接近哈维的地盘。 OpenAI 是 Harvey 的早期投资者。

在采访中，萨姆·奥尔特曼经常将自己的公司描述为其他初创公司赖以发展的基础，而不是为每个行业提供应用程序的竞争对手。 他在 3 月份的贝莱德活动舞台上表示：“我们看到，未来智能将成为像电或水一样的公用事业，人们通过电表从我们这里购买它，然后将其用于他们想要的任何用途。”

法律可能会表明 OpenAI 是否满足于出售电力，或者是否也想制造这些设备。

它在水里

一个悬而未决的问题是，OpenAI 进入其主要竞​​争对手已经在研究的领域是否为时已晚。

“绝对不是，”扎克·阿布拉莫维茨（Zach Abramowitz）在接受采访时表示，他是一名顾问，为律师事务所提供技术如何颠覆其行业的建议。

他的理由很简单：律师可能还没有购买“ChatGPT for Law”，但他们中的许多人已经是 ChatGPT 的高级用户。 Law360 最近的一项调查发现，律师使用人工智能的频率比一年前要高得多。 自我报告使用率最高的工具是 ChatGPT，占受访者的 54%，领先于 Microsoft Copilot、Gemini、Harvey 和 Claude。

内部采用情况更难追踪，但吸引力可能更大。 公司法律团队有充分的动力将更多工作自动化，而且许多人都坐在公司内部，工程师、客户支持以及中间的每个人都已经在使用 ChatGPT 或 Claude。

ChatGTP 于 2022 年发布后的最初几周内，数百万用户中包括律师在内。 他们尝试向其寻求法律建议（答案范围从“令人惊讶的好”到“危险的错误”）并通过律师资格考试（其正确率达到 50.3%，高于随机猜测率）。 正如一位律师当时所写的那样，总体结果“对于人类律师来说并不令人印象深刻”，但对于机器来说“相当惊人”。

律师事务所高管 Oz Benamram 在他的新书《法律，重塑》中写道，这些实验共同指出了许多律师以前没有认真考虑过的事情。 人工智能已经足够好，可以承担部分法律工作。

科技创始人也敲响了警钟。

那一年，Harvey 的创始人给当时 OpenAI 的总法律顾问 Jason Kwon 发了一封冷邮件，相信这些模型可以经过调整，使法律研究、起草和其他律师任务的速度快得离谱。 如今，Harvey 为超过 100,000 名法律专业人士提供服务，估值达 110 亿美元。

哈维成为了更大的淘金热的证据。 从旧金山到斯德哥尔摩，创始人竞相将大型语言模型打包为律师应用程序。 根据 Crunchbase 的数据，2026 年上半年，全球法律科技初创公司的融资额达到 21 亿美元。

Anthropic 一头扎进了这一类别。 五月份，竞争对手实验室发布了 Claude for Legal，这是一个针对律师事务所和内部法律顾问的插件库。 它针对的是该行业的繁重工作：查找案例、审查合同以及传送文件和请求。

与此同时，帮助创造法律技术繁荣的公司基本上没有参与下一章。

OpenAI 已将 ChatGPT Enterprise 出售给越来越多的律师事务所，去年，该公司在博客中介绍了其员工用来发现问题并从合同中提取信息的内部工具。 但该系统是为了帮助 OpenAI 运营其业务而构建的，而不是作为律师产品发布。

“欢迎来到聚会”

Boehmig 七周前加入 OpenAI。 他已经组建了一支乐队。

7 月，Stacey Wang 离开 Ironclad 前往 OpenAI，领导法律和专业服务的行业营销。 在社交媒体帖子中，两人都表示正在与 Sherwin Wu 密切合作，Sherwin Wu 在 OpenAI Platform 负责 B2B Verticals 的工程工作，该业务允许其他公司使用 OpenAI 的模型构建产品。

OpenAI 还配备了人员来回答一个更基本的问题：它的模型是否真的足以胜任法律工作？ 4月，该公司聘请了一位前公司律师作为法律评估专家。 人工智能实验室使用评估来衡量他们的系统在现实世界任务中的表现，这样他们就可以判断每个新版本是否真的变得更好。

这些招聘加剧了法律科技领域一直存在的恐惧。 模范公司可能不满足于为其他人的软件提供支持。 他们可能也想要工作流程。

Harvey 的创始人兼首席执行官温斯顿·温伯格 (Winston Weinberg) 已经习惯了这种紧张气氛。 甚至在 OpenAI 开始配备法律人员之前，温伯格就告诉《商业内幕》，Harvey 总是会与 OpenAI 最后发布的任何广泛使用的工具进行比较。

在最近接受《商业内幕》采访时，温伯格似乎很冷静，尽管哈维正在为其最重要的合作伙伴之一可能成为更直接挑战者的未来做准备。 他的观点是，每个新进入者都可以通过吸引更多人关注法律人工智能并向律师展示该技术的用途来帮助哈维。

他向 OpenAI 传达的信息是：“欢迎来到聚会。”
